title: Power BI Dashboard with Healthcare Data
description: Healthcare insurance data is growing and growing but delivering quick insight for strategic business planning may not be a easy task. With the combination of power bi self service platform and SQL  Server databases you'll be on the right path to new data discoveries!! Follow this step by step tutorial on how to create the best customizable visualizations with endless discovery possibilities. This guide will help  you build out proper metrics, and corresponding visualizations.
stepByStepContent:
Power BI Dashboard with Healthcare Data
Healthcare insurance data is growing and growing but delivering quick insight for strategic business planning may not be a easy task. With the combination of power bi self service platform and SQL Server databases you'll be on the right path to new data discoveries!! Follow this step by step tutorial on how to create the best customizable visualizations with endless discovery possibilities. This guide will help you build out proper metrics, and corresponding visualizations.
Step Name:How Does a Completed Dashboard Look?
Detailed Instructions:

Dashboard

Finalize the layout by adding and placing Images  that represent the entities and process(es)  involved in the business process that the report is based off of. Usually this involves a  Transaction, a Location, People, and a Service/Product.  In our case, three dimension tables were involved (Groups, Members, Plans) as well as one Fact table (Memberships). Be sure to also add an Image of the company logo and  a text box to display the company name. Finally, add a color theme to the report visualizations. Here the ‘Electric’ color theme was implemented to match the colors of the company logo. 

Insight: 

Power BI Dashboards should be designed to tell a story through visualizations and contain only the most important elements according to the BRD

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y4sRWbo8JcE&t=5s

Did you complete the Project?

allStepDetails.content:
Power BI Dashboard with Healthcare Data
Healthcare insurance data is growing and growing but delivering quick insight for strategic business planning may not be a easy task. With the combination of power bi self service platform and SQL Server databases you'll be on the right path to new data discoveries!! Follow this step by step tutorial on how to create the best customizable visualizations with endless discovery possibilities. This guide will help you build out proper metrics, and corresponding visualizations.
Step Name:How to Create the Data Mart– From T-SQL Script
Detailed Instructions:

Power BI supports large range of data sources. You can click "Get Data" and it shows you all the available data connections. It allows you to connect to different flat files, SQL database, and Azure cloud or even web platforms such as Facebook, Google Analytics, and Salesforce objects.

 

On this project the Data Source is SQL Server to load the data into Power BI 

 

Open SQL Server Management Studio and connect to proper SQL Server instance using the appropriate SQL Server authentication credentials.

 

Open a New Query window. Execute the script provided with the .sql file to create the [HealthCare Insurance] Data Mart. The [HealthCare Insurance] Data Mart stores historical data relating to membership enrollment.

The image below displays the Database Diagram that corresponds with the HealthCare Insurance Data Mart. This displays the one-to-many relationships between the [Fact HealthCare] table and the related dimension tables. 

Insight: 

  Download Power BI, if not installed. 

https://powerbi.microsoft.com/en-us/get-started/?&OCID=AID719832_SEM_M2kNt4pL&lnkd=Google_PowerBI_Brand&gclid=Cj0KCQjw1pblBRDSARIsACfUG11CaX5Dpn0-vx8ucUtHhNGUQWbZB4plK5ef_wcdkUF5wtPT1hG8MzkaAmeTEALw_wcB

Step Name:How to Upload Data Mart From SQL Server Into Power BI
Detailed Instructions:

 Open Power BI Desktop click on "Get Data"

Select SQL Server and the below window will open

 

Fill in the below information to direct Power BI on where to import the dataset from.

 

Once you select the database Power BI will import the tables as below

Above is the preview of the tables selected for the report dataset.

 

Select the tables required for your reports

Then click on “Load”. 

 

Insight: 

  Save a copy of the report on your computer as a .PBIX file. 

  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Qgam9M8I0xA

This guide will help you build out proper metrics, and corresponding visualizations.
Step Name:How to Create and Use Your Own Measures
Detailed Instructions:

How to Create and use your own measures

With Power BI Desktop, you can create your own measures with the Data Analysis Expressions (DAX) formula language, any field that appears with a sigma icon  is numeric, and its values can be aggregated, Sum is the default aggregation for a numeric datatype, but you can easily apply different aggregations like average or count. 

Note:  Actual measure for NumOfMembers below:

NumOfMembers = DISTINCTCOUNT('Fact_HealthCare'[MemberKey])

(Note: The above measure has been uploaded for your convenience)

Below are additional measures you may create for additional visualizations: 

(Note: You are not required to create measures as a file has been provided along with final dashboard at end of presentation for your convenience)

Insight: 

Measures are ideal for aggregation to a value (i.e. sum, average, counts etc.)

 

   https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KSylKLX_OC4

portfolioContent.title: Power BI Dashboard with Healthcare Data
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a dynamic Power BI dashboard integrating healthcare insurance data from SQL Server to enable strategic business insights. Created customizable visualizations and key metrics to support data-driven decision making.
portfolioContent.summary: This project involved building a comprehensive Power BI dashboard using healthcare insurance data sourced from SQL Server databases. The dashboard features custom measures, slicers, and various visualizations designed to provide actionable insights for strategic planning. The step-by-step tutorial guides users through data import, metric creation, and visualization best practices.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Healthcare insurance data is rapidly expanding, making it challenging for organizations to quickly extract meaningful insights for strategic business planning. Traditional reporting methods often lack flexibility and interactivity, limiting the ability to explore data effectively. There was a need for a self-service BI solution that enables users to create customizable and insightful visualizations efficiently.
objectives: Connect and import healthcare insurance data from SQL Server into Power BI.
Create custom metrics and measures using DAX to analyze membership data.
Design interactive and visually appealing dashboards to support strategic decision making.
skills: SQL querying and data mart creation
workflow: Connect to SQL Server and execute scripts to create data marts.
Import required tables into Power BI Desktop using Get Data feature.
Create custom measures and calculated fields with DAX.
Build interactive visualizations including slicers, cards, pie charts, and stacked bar charts.
Finalize dashboard layout with images, themes, and company branding.
keyInsights: Power BI supports seamless integration with SQL Server for efficient data import.
Custom DAX measures enable precise aggregation and analysis of healthcare membership data.
Interactive slicers and visualizations enhance user-driven data exploration.
Effective dashboard design focuses on storytelling by highlighting key business metrics.
businessImpact: Accelerated data-driven strategic planning through interactive dashboards.
Improved accessibility of healthcare insurance insights for business users.
Enabled scalable and customizable reporting to adapt to evolving business needs.
